data.title: Commodity Roundup: Copper futures extend rally; China’s lithium reserves world’s second-largest
data.description:
Copper futures (HG1:COM) extended their winning streak +1.33% to a fourth trading session on Thursday, as investors continued to weigh the outlook for China, while the country also became the world's second-largest holder of lithium reserves.

The consumer price index rose 0.1% in December from a year earlier, while factory deflation extended into a 27th month, though the producer price index recorded a slower drop of 2.3%, the National Bureau of Statistics said Thursday.

However, some market participants remain optimistic that China will follow through on its recent commitments to ramp up monetary and fiscal stimulus to support economic growth.

Meanwhile, China’s lithium reserves have risen from 6% to 16.5% of the global total, making it the world’s second-largest holder of lithium reserves, state media reported on Wednesday.

The newly discovered mines include a 2 800-km-long spodumene mine in the Xikunsong-Pan-Ganzi region in Tibet, and some lithium salt lakes in the Qinghai-Tibet Plateau, Xinhua News Agency said in the report.

Potentially relevant tickers include (ALB), (SQM), (LAC), (LAAC), (ALTM), (PLL), (SLI), (OTCPK:PILBF)

ETF: (NYSEARCA:LIT), (BATT)

Turning to the physical gold market in the second-biggest buyer, India has slashed its November gold import estimates by an unprecedented $5 billion, the largest revision for any commodity in history, after errors in preliminary calculations inflated the figure to a record, Reuters reported.

New Delhi reported last month that its gold imports surged to a record high of $14.8 billion in November, more than doubling from $7.13 billion in October.

Elsewhere, Federal Reserve officials indicated that the central bank will have to take a “careful approach” in cutting interest rates further due to the rising risk that inflation will remain persistently higher than its 2% target.

ETFs: (GLD), (GDX), (GDXJ), (IAU), (NUGT), (PHYS), (GLDM), (AAAU), (SGOL), (RING), (BAR), (OUNZ), (SLV), (PSLV), (SIVR), (SIL), (SILJ)

data.title: Should stock investors be afraid of rising Treasury yields?
data.description:
The stock market is closed Thursday for former President Jimmy Carter's state funeral, but bond markets are open until 2 PM ET. Interesting things have been happening in the fixed-income world over the past few months as the Fed embarked on an easing cycle. All three interest rate cuts by the central bank since September have seen a subsequent INCREASE in longer-term Treasury yields (though those are more associated with the longer-term monetary and fiscal landscape, as well as economic conditions).

Snapshot: The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) rose to 4.73% on Wednesday, a whole percentage point higher than the level seen only a quarter ago, while the 20-year Treasury (US20Y) topped 5% for the first time since 2023. Concerns over the national debt and debt ceiling are at play, along with possible inflationary threats like higher tariffs, tax cuts, and mass deportations. Worries over fiscal discipline are also not restricted to the U.S., with borrowing costs in France now above those of Greece, while gilt yields in the U.K. have been pushed to their highest levels since 2008.

Investors are demanding larger premiums in response to the uncertainty, but there are also risks that this can turn into bond market activism or even so-called "bond vigilantism." In the current environment, central banks have stepped back from their role as a dominant force in bond markets, which was conducted via quantitative easing following the financial crisis. That has given additional power to fund managers and other market players, leaving many wondering what it might mean for equity markets.

SA commentary: "While stocks may struggle during periods when long-term rates are rising, that struggle should be temporary so long as the increase is for good reason," writes SA Investing Group Leader Lawrence Fuller. "As we have become accustomed in the past two years, the pessimists and fearmongers are warning of doom and gloom ahead, but markets are simply adjusting to the normalization of the yield curve, as the economic expansion continues." Read more in Rates Are Rising For The Right Reason, So Don't Panic

More on bonds

  • Return of the bond vigilantes? Sharp gains for U.S. yields
  • Bond Market Massacre: Could Surging Rates End The Epic Bull Market For Stocks?
  • US10Y soars about 100 bps since Fed rate cuts, signaling a diverging inflation outlook
  • SocGen sees sign of bonds becoming more attractive to hold vs. stocks
  • Yellen decodes recent yield climb, pointing to term premium, rate repricing

data.title: Vanguard Group raises stake in Talen Energy
data.description:
Vanguard Group has raised its stake in Talen Energy (NASDAQ:TLN) to 10.41%, a regulatory filing showed on Wednesday.

The investment manager purchased over 4M shares of the utility's common stock, representing a 10.41% interest. It held about 9.9% of its outstanding shares as of October.

The additional investment comes as Big Tech firms turn to utilities to power their AI data centers.

Shares of Talen Energy (TLN) have jumped around 240% over the past year and are rated as “Strong Buy” by Wall Street analysts.

data.title: China's Xi Jinping said to silence economist for doubting GDP data
data.description:
China's President Xi Jinping has silenced prominent economist Gao Shanwen after he raised doubts about the reliability of official GDP data and Beijing’s ability to help bolster economic growth, The Wall Street Journal reported, citing people familiar with the matter.

"We do not know the true number of China's real growth figure and maybe some other numbers," Gao said at an event in Washington last month. "My own speculation is that in the past 2-3 years, the real number on average might be around 2%, even though the official number is close to 5%."

"If my speculation is correct, I think it might be more reasonable to expect a growth rate between 3%-4% in the next 3-5 years, but we know the official number will always be around 5%."

Gao, chief economist at state-run SDIC Securities, also said Beijing's efforts to stimulate the economy will be very opportunistic. "In the end, I don't think they can very confidently deliver what they have promised."

His statements reportedly angered Xi, who ordered an investigation of Gao. The economist has been banned from speaking publicly, but has been allowed to keep his job.

Xi's reaction sheds light on how Beijing has been clamping down on any criticism related to the prolonged economic slowdown in China.

Another economist, Zhu Hengpeng, was placed under investigation last year for allegedly criticizing Xi's management of the economy in a private chat. Zhu was then detained and removed from his post at the state-run Chinese Academy of Social Sciences, WSJ reported.

Last month, the Securities Association of China warned brokerages to ensure their economists play a positive role in analyzing government policies or face the risk of getting fired.

data.title: KKR extends second tender offer for Japanese software developer Fuji Soft
data.description:
• KKR (NYSE:KKR) announced on Thursday the extension of its second tender offer for the common shares and share options of the Japanese software developer Fuji Soft.
  • The end date of the tender offer period has been extended from January 9 to January 24.
  • The private equity firm has submitted an amendment statement to the Tender Offer Registration Statement submitted on November 20, 2024.
  • The tender offer price per share will remain at ¥9,451.
  • Investment firm Bain Capital had also proposed to acquire Fuji Soft.
  • KKR believes there will not be a tender offer by Bain Capital that investors can tender into, and said it continues to be committed to Fuji Soft's privatization.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Acer reports 9.7% rise in FY revenue
data.description:
• Acer (OTC:ACEYY) reported a 9.7% rise in its full year 2024 preliminary revenues.
  • The firm said its revenues came in at NT$264.71 billion, with revenues from the personal computers and display business growing by 8.4% Y/Y.
  • For Q4, the firm said its revenues touched NT$66.12 billion, with 4.7% growth Y/Y.
  • The total revenues from businesses other than computers and displays contributed 29.3% in Q4 2024 with 14.2% Y/Y growth.

data.title: Dutch government in talks with Nvidia for AI facility in the Netherlands - report
data.description:
The government of the Netherlands is in discussions with Nvidia (NASDAQ:NVDA) and Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) to support the setting up of an AI facility in the country, Bloomberg News reported.

The facility will be aimed at the development and application of AI with the help of a supercomputer, the report added, citing a statement. "Discussions were held with Nvidia about the delivery of hardware and support with knowledge in creating the required infrastructure."

The Dutch government has reached a deal with Nvidia for the supply of hardware and technological knowledge for the potential AI facility, according to a report from Reuters.

Nvidia and AMD have not yet responded to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.

Dutch Economic Affairs Minister Dirk Beljaarts is on a visit to the U.S. to boost cooperation between technology companies of the two countries.

Beljaarts said the chances of realizing the project grew significantly through the agreement with Nvidia, without providing details, the report added.

"Competition is fierce, the whole world is after this technology," said Beljaarts following a meeting with Nvidia, the report noted.

data.title: Euro Area retail sales tick 1.2% higher in November
data.description:
Retail sales in the Euro Area increased 0.10% in November over the previous month, missing consensus estimates of 0.4%. 

Y/Y retail sales increased 1.20% in November over the same month in the previous year. 

Trade volume for food, drinks, tobacco increased by 0.1%, non-food products (except automotive fuel) was 0.6% higher, automotive fuel in specialized stores was up by 0.8%.

data.title: Adentra strengthens leadership with board additions, COO appointment
data.description:
• Adentra (TSX:ADEN:CA) Wednesday reported the retirement of founding director Graham Wilson, new board appointments, and the promotion of Drew Dickinson to Chief Operating Officer.
  • Rob Taylor, who has been a director of Adentra since 2019, will become Chair of the Board effective immediately.
  • Drew Dickinson, with over 30 years of industry and leadership experience, has been promoted to Chief Operating Officer of Adentra.
  • Dickinson served as COO of Rugby Architectural Building Products prior to its acquisition by Adentra, President of Rugby, and most recently as President of Adentra's U.S. Industrial business.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Zoomcar reports 17% Y/Y growth in December 2024 bookings
data.description:
• India-based car sharing marketplace Zoomcar Holdings (NASDAQ:ZCAR) announced on Thursday a 17% year-over-year growth in bookings for December 2024.
  • The company said it recorded its highest-ever unaudited and unreviewed contribution profit for December 2024, amounting to $494.51K based on provisional financials for the month.
  • Source: Press Release
